commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs maintainer choice on the toolFilter runtime contract before automation can safely repair or merge the PR.; Needs attention: The PR introduces a tool-selection control that can be bypassed by sibling MCP projection paths unless the contract is enforced or scoped before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Honor tool filters before projecting native MCP servers — src/config/mcp-config.ts:100-103 (sha=374a0905dce2c9e1df1536d6ed1464e581226ce4)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds MCP status/probe/tools CLI support, a new per-server `toolFilter` config surface, resource/prompt utility tools, cache invalidation, failure backoff, tests, and docs.

PR surface: Source +863, Tests +784, Docs +34. Total +1681 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the PR persists `toolFilter` globally and applies it in the embedded runtime, while current sibling MCP projection paths do not enforce that selection.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer choice on the `toolFilter` runtime contract before automation can safely repair or merge the PR.

target.title: Improve MCP operability
target.body:
## Summary

- Add MCP status/probe/tools CLI support for transport inspection, live capability probing, and per-server tool selection.
- Expose MCP resources/prompts through generated utility tools, with shared filtering, diagnostics, prompt-safe metadata handling, and inventory parity.
- Harden bundled MCP runtime behavior around best-effort tool listing, list-changed cache invalidation, refresh diagnostics, request failure backoff, and config schema/help/docs.

## Verification

- pnpm test src/cli/mcp-cli.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
- pnpm build
- live CLI proof with a temp stdio MCP server: set/status/tools/show/probe verified stdio transport, toolFilter persistence, filtered tool projection, resources/prompts, and listChanged capabilities
- git diff --check
- pnpm docs:list
- env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: MCP operators can inspect configured transports without connecting, probe configured servers with live diagnostics, filter per-server tools, see resource/prompt utilities, and avoid stale or runaway failing MCP operations.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on macOS with the built OpenClaw CLI and a real temporary stdio MCP server process.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the CLI, configured a temp stdio MCP server with openclaw mcp set, checked openclaw mcp status --json, wrote a filter with openclaw mcp tools, verified config with openclaw mcp show --json, and connected with openclaw mcp probe --json.
Evidence after fix: probe reported one exposed tool, one filtered tool, resources=true, prompts=true, listChanged true for tools/resources/prompts, and projected tools ["live__search_docs"].
Observed result after fix: the filtered MCP tool remained available under a provider-safe OpenClaw tool name while admin and generated utility tools were hidden by the filter; status remained non-connecting.
What was not tested: remote Testbox changed-gate execution; the delegated Testbox checkout failed before checks with no merge base for origin/main...HEAD, while the same changed gate passed locally without Testbox delegation.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
